Ad Text:
About this Opportunity

Fixed-Term (until 31.12.2026), Full-Time

Digital Services Directorate

CQUniversity Rockhampton (other Central Queensland locations by negotiation, excl. SEQ region)

Higher Education Worker Level 7

Total Remuneration $121,744 to $131,995 pa (including salary of $102,876 to $111,538 plus 17% superannuation and leave loading)

Join an organisation with an unbreakable belief that quality education, training and research should be available to everyone – regardless of background, location or life circumstances.

Working at CQUniversity

The Digital Services Directorate drives the planning, development, support and continuous improvement of CQUniversity’s business-critical digital systems. As part of this team, you will play a vital role in planning, developing, and supporting CQUniversity’s GenAI-powered systems, which are critical to the University’s future operations.

Your work will directly contribute to driving positive value for CQUniversity and enhancing the digital experience for both students and staff.

About the Role

In this role, you will contribute to CQUniversity’s GenAI strategy and roadmap by creating and assessing architecture and solution designs, developing GenAI applications, and supporting innovation through the implementation of GenAI proof-of-concepts and prototypes. You will also foster the development of GenAI technical capability across our teams by providing advice and support to fellow developers.

We are seeking a proactive professional with extensive experience in software development and architecture, a passion for emerging technologies, and a strong interest in applying GenAI to real-world challenges. You will be working with the latest technology in the Microsoft AI stack, so knowledge and experience with these platforms is highly desirable. Strong communication and organisational skills, and the ability to work effectively as part of a team, are essential.

Rockhampton is a thriving regional metropolis with a strong economy, excellent schools, a vibrant arts and culture scene, and an excellent range of sporting and recreational opportunities and major events. With CQU’s flexible and supportive conditions enjoy a great balance of work, life and play with affordable housing, mild climate and friendly people. Nestled along the Fitzroy River, with breathtaking riverfront views and opportunities for outdoor activities like fishing, boating and kayaking.  The nearby Capricorn Coast is home to pristine beaches, national parks, and is the gateway to the Southern Great Barrier Reef. 

Based at our Rockhampton campus, or Central Queensland locations by negotiation, other you will join a collaborative, agile team where your work will help shape innovation in higher education.

Be Rewarded

17% superannuation (with the choice to join an award-winning superfund)

6 weeks annual leave (incl. Christmas and New Year closure)

10 days personal/sick leave, plus 5 days carer’s leave

7.25 hours engaged service leave

Fantastic professional and career development opportunities

Up to 26 weeks (six months) paid parental leave

Access to an Employee Assistance Program and Fitness Passport

Position Requirements

Essential

A detailed understanding of modern generative and agentic AI frameworks and standards.

A detailed understanding of programming languages, web development and agile development practices.

Demonstrated time/task management and project coordination skills.

Exceptional communication skills.

Desirable

Experience in GenAI application development and systems integration.

For further information about this position, please refer to the Position Description on the CQUniversity Careers Site.

WorkSafe is Victoria's workplace health and safety regulator. We are also the workplace injury insurer. Our job is to reduce workplace harm and improve outcomes for injured workers. This is our promise to the Victorian community and the reason we exist.





Integration Solution Designer

$152,556.00 per annum + 12% superannuation
Full time, ongoing opportunity
Wadawurrung/Geelong based role (hybrid work from home - 3 days a week in office)

About the opportunity

Business Unit - Strategic Delivery, Technology Operations

At WorkSafe Victoria, our purpose is clear: to reduce workplace harm and improve outcomes for injured workers. This is our promise to the Victorian community. Our Technology Operations team plays a vital role in delivering robust data and integration platforms that power insights, analytics, and decision-making to support safer workplaces and better recovery for injured workers.

This is an exciting opportunity for a skilled Integration Solution Designer to shape and deliver high-impact data and integration solutions. You will create detailed design artefacts, lead technical specifications for cloud-based data platforms, and provide expert guidance to ensure solutions are secure, scalable, and aligned with enterprise architecture. Working in a dynamic environment, you will drive innovation in integration frameworks while contributing to our goal of meaningful change for Victorians.

Key accountabilities:

You will be designing and leading the detailed technical design of complex integration and data solutions.
You will lead troubleshooting, specification, and implementation to ensure compatibility and supportability.
You will create high-quality design documentation and provide specialist advice that balances functional, security, and performance needs.

Please ensure you review the position description here for additional information on accountabilities for this role.

About you

You are an experienced integration and data solutions professional who wants to make a difference in workplace safety and community outcomes. Your communication and organisational skills are excellent. You have a passion for translating complex technical requirements into practical, high-quality designs that deliver real value.

You bring proven expertise in designing and delivering data/integration solutions, with a strong focus on frontend analytics tools and robust querying capabilities. You thrive in independent and collaborative settings, applying expert knowledge to end-to-end problem resolution.

Mandatory skills and experience/must have:

Strong expertise in SAS and WebFOCUS for data analytics, reporting, and visualisation.
Advanced SQL skills for complex querying, data transformation, and performance optimisation across large datasets.
Demonstrated ITIL knowledge (v3 or v4 certification highly desirable) to support service management, incident resolution, and delivery frameworks.
Experience designing solutions using cloud data engineering tools (e.g., SAS EM, AWS Glue/Lambda, Azure Data Factory/Synapse) and ETL platforms (e.g., SAS Data Integration Studio, Informatica or Talend).
Proven ability to produce detailed design artefacts, conduct impact analysis, and lead technical reviews for integration and data components.

Working with us

At WorkSafe, our goal is for every employee to feel connected, safe, and valued within their team, for teams to feel appreciated in the workplace, and for WorkSafe to be respected in the community.

We believe that a diverse workforce is essential for delivering the best outcomes for the Victorian community. We strongly encourage applications from all individuals, including First Nations people, people with disabilities, those from diverse cultural and linguistic backgrounds, and individuals of all sexualities and genders.

What's next?

Applications close 11:00pm Thursday 26th March 2026. Click the link to apply, and join us in making a difference to Victorian lives.





Only applications submitted via the WorkSafe Careers website will be considered.

Preferred candidates will be required to satisfactorily complete a Nationally Coordinated Criminal History Check and/or other checks as required for the position as part of the recruitment process.

Company Description


Nearmap is the Australian-founded, global tech pioneer innovating the location intelligence game. Customers rely on Nearmap for consistent, reliable, high-resolution imagery, insights, and answers to create meaningful change in the world and propel industries forward.

Harnessing its own patented camera systems, imagery capture, AI, geospatial tools, and advanced SaaS platforms, Nearmap stands as the definitive source of truth that shapes the livable world.

Job Description


Transform aerial capture systems with cutting-edge embedded solutions

Nearmap is seeking a Senior Embedded Systems Engineer to join our innovative Capture Systems team. Work at the intersection of embedded systems and aerial imaging technology, developing real-time solutions that power our next-generation capture systems. You'll contribute across the full embedded systems lifecycle- from R&D through to production and manufacturing.

Work on sophisticated embedded systems that power our aerial imagery capture platforms used worldwide. You'll be integral to developing real-time solutions for current and future capture system components, contributing across the entire lifecycle from initial R&D through qualification, production, and manufacturing. This is a rare opportunity to work on complex mechatronic systems where precision and reliability are critical.

What You'll Be Working On

Real-Time Embedded Systems: Specify, develop, document, and qualify embedded systems solutions for aerial capture platforms, with a focus on bare-metal development and deterministic performance
High-Precision Motor Control: Research, develop, and qualify advanced motor control solutions that demand ultra-high precision for aerial imagery capture systems
Hardware-Software Integration: Interface embedded software with sophisticated electronic hardware systems, ensuring seamless operation in real-world aerial environments
Verification & Validation: Develop and execute comprehensive testing strategies, both cross-platform and on target hardware, to ensure system reliability and performance
Control Systems Development: Design and implement linear and non-linear feedback controllers for electro-mechanical applications, combining theoretical modelling with practical implementation
Cross-Disciplinary Collaboration: Work closely with multi-disciplinary Technology and Capture Systems teams, contributing to knowledge sharing and driving innovation across the organisation

Who You Are

Excellent communicator with strong verbal, written, and visual communication skills
Positive self-starter who thrives in collaborative, team-oriented environments
Comfortable working in fast-paced, dynamic settings where priorities can shift
High emotional intelligence with the ability to work effectively across different teams and disciplines
Detail-oriented problem solver who can balance theoretical knowledge with practical implementation
Passionate about embedded systems engineering and eager to tackle complex technical challenges

Qualifications


Essential:

8+ years embedded systems engineering experience with bare-metal development focus
Real-time/deterministic software development expertise
Hardware-software interfacing and embedded verification/validation experience
Strong algorithmic development skills for mechatronic control systems
Experience with feedback controller design and implementation for electro-mechanical applications
Bachelor's degree in Computer/Electronic/Electrical/Mechatronic Engineering or related field

Highly Regarded:

10+ years embedded systems experience
ARM Cortex-M7 architecture expertise
Ultra high-precision mechatronic systems background
Control systems theory and practical implementation experience

Please note this is an in office role, based in Sydney and therefore cannot cater to remote employees. 

Additional Information


Some of our benefits

Nearmap takes a holistic approach to our employees’ emotional, physical and financial wellness. Some of our current benefits include:

Quarterly wellbeing day off - Four additional days off annually for your 'YOU' Days
Wellbeing and technology allowance
Annual flu vaccinations
Nearmap subscription (of course!)
Stocked kitchen with access to all the snacks you need
In-office lunch every Tuesday and Thursday at our Sydney CBD office
Showers available for anyone cycling to work or lunchtime gym-goers!

Working at Nearmap
We move fast and work smart; often wearing multiple hats. We adapted to remote working with ease and are continually looking at ways to improve. We’re proud of our inclusive, supportive culture, and maintain a safe environment where everyone feels a sense of belonging and can be themselves.

More about Nearmap AI:  

Ready to elevate your embedded systems career? Apply now.

Position: Integration Analyst (multiple opportunities)

Applications Close: 11:59pm, 15 March 2026

About Us – Single Digital Patient Record (SDPR), NSW Health
Be part of the team leading the largest digital transformation in Australia’s public health history. NSW Health’s SDPR program is transforming the way patient care is delivered and experienced - creating a single, secure, holistic, and integrated view of patient information across the entire state. 

By connecting every corner of the NSW public health system, this groundbreaking program empowers clinicians and care teams with real-time access to unified patient data - enabling safer, more transparent, and quality care for every person, anywhere. 

Join us now and contribute to the millions of ways we're enriching health every day.
 

About the Opportunity

As an Integration Analyst of our Technology, Devices and Integration team within SDPRIA, you will oversee the planning, coordination and implementation of activities that support the integration of key applications and systems across NSW Health. 

This role offers the opportunity to contribute to large-scale state-wide programs through the analysis, development and support of system interfaces and integration solutions. You’ll work with messaging protocols, data models, and integration platforms to enable seamless information flow and support key digital health outcomes. 

In this role, you will:

Collaborate with technical and clinical stakeholders to deliver integration solutions that meet program needs
Support programs through the analysis, development and maintenance of application interfaces and technical workflows
Contribute to the resolution of complex incidents impacting integration architecture and ensure alignment with service-level expectations
Document business and technical requirements through integration data modelling and process mapping
Advise on integration-related risks, upgrades and changes to ensure system integrity and data security 

About You

We are looking for someone who:

Has experience working within a healthcare integration team supporting clinical systems and digital health programs
Has strong experience in business process analysis, data modelling and integration documentation
Brings a problem-solving mindset, with the ability to investigate and resolve complex technical incidents
Possesses excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills in a fast-paced, multidisciplinary environment
Holds relevant qualifications or equivalent experience in integration support within a large and complex organisation
Demonstrates a strong understanding of integration security practices and protocols 

Nice to have:

Experience developing interfaces using integration engines such as Rhapsody, InterSystems IRIS, Mirth Connect, MuleSoft, or similar platforms
Competence supporting production integrations, integration engines, or EMR integration modules
 

You will succeed in this role if you: 

Enjoy working in an environment where no two days are the same. 
Are motivated by tight deadlines and problem solving on the go. 
Can juggle multiple priorities while staying calm and outcome focused. 
Thrive when collaborating with others to deliver results in a fast-moving space.  

 

Need more information?

Position description

Working at SDPRIA

Our people are at the heart of who we are. We support our people with great benefits so they can enrich health in millions of ways every day:

Professional growth and career development

Share with us your cover letter and most up to date resume (up to 5 pages), highlighting your relevant skills and experience. 

We recognise that AI tools are increasingly being used to assist with resumes and applications. While we welcome their use, we’re eager to see your personality and get to know the person behind the application!

General recruitment and adjustment enquiries: If you require assistance, have any questions about the recruitment or require adjustments to the recruitment/interview process, please contact Vaaruni Narayana at Vaaruni.narayanaiyengar@health.nsw.gov.au

Ad Text:
About the Role

Join LGI’s Projects team as an Industrial Automation Specialist and support the delivery and operation of LGI’s renewable energy facilities, including landfill gas power generation, flare and gas processing systems.

This hands-on role focuses on installation verification, commissioning and troubleshooting of industrial instrumentation and control systems across LGI facilities. You will work closely with the Senior Control Systems Engineer, supporting commissioning of new projects and optimisation of control systems at operational sites.

This Brisbane-based role includes travel to project sites across Australia.

Key Accountabilities/Responsibilities 

Instrumentation & Control System Commissioning: Verify installation and commission instrumentation and control systems, including loop checks, calibration and field verification.

Automation System Integration: Support integration and commissioning of PLC, SCADA and HMI systems, including configuration, communications and system testing.

Testing, Fault Finding & Operational Support: Assist with FAT and SAT testing, troubleshoot instrumentation and control system faults, and support commissioning of VFDs, motor control systems and automation equipment.

Documentation & Engineering Support: Maintain control system documentation, including loop diagrams, wiring drawings, instrument registers and commissioning records.

What We're Looking For

Hands-on experience commissioning industrial instrumentation or control systems

Experience with PLC platforms (Siemens, Panasonic, Schneider or similar)

Experience working with SCADA systems

Experience with instrumentation installation, loop testing and commissioning

Strong troubleshooting skills in industrial automation environments

Highly regarded:

Experience in power generation or industrial energy facilities

Experience with gas processing or process plant environments

Exposure to FAT and SAT testing for industrial equipment

Experience with industrial communication protocols such as Modbus, Ethernet/IP, Profibus or MQTT

Qualifications

Diploma / Advanced Diploma in Electrical or Instrumentation Engineering, OR

Bachelor’s Degree in Electrical, Instrumentation or Mechatronics Engineering, OR

Electrical / Instrumentation trade qualification with relevant industrial experience

Additional Requirements

Willingness to travel to project sites when required

Current Australian driver's licence

Strong practical troubleshooting and problem-solving skills

Ability to work safely in industrial environments

What We Offer

Competitive salary package

Career development and training opportunities

Exposure to renewable power generation and industrial automation projects

Supportive engineering team environment

Flexible working arrangements where project activities allow

About LGI

LGI develops and operates renewable energy projects that convert landfill gas into electricity, helping reduce emissions and support Australia’s transition to cleaner energy.

Apply Now

You must have permanent work rights in Australia. Sponsorship is not available for this role.

Location: Sydney, Australia (Hybrid)

We're working with a well-funded AI company that solves real client problems. They're building agentic systems, deploying multi-agent frameworks and working with clients who have genuinely challenging problems to solve.
  
They are now looking for Senior AI Engineers to keep up with demand. Ideally, you should be an experienced Data Scientist (at least 3 years of experience) with a deep technical background in NLP or deep learning, including recent experience productionising GenAI and agentic AI projects.

You’ll work in an agile environment with multi-disciplinary teams, including some of the most technical and experienced professionals in this field. Your key responsibilities will include (but not limited to):

Motorola Solutions is a global leader in mission-critical communications and analytics. Our platforms in public safety and enterprise security help customers stay safe, secure, and productive.

The Post-Sale Systems Engineering team is the technical life and blood of Motorola Solutions Systems Integration Organisation. Our engineers are focused on designing and implementing complex communication solutions for Solving for Safer We act as solution architects and technical consultants, managing the entire post-sale project lifecycle from concept to deployment, including design, consultancy, and implementation. Our commitment is to deliver world-class, end-to-end solutions for Mission Critical Enterprise and Government customers, prioritising reliability, resilience, and performance for critical communication.

Job Description

We are seeking an experienced and highly motivated Senior Staff Systems Engineer to lead a team of talented systems engineers. The successful candidate will be responsible for the technical leadership, management, and growth of the systems engineering team, ensuring the delivery of high-quality, innovative solutions that meet customer requirements.

Responsibilities

Lead and manage a team of systems engineers, providing technical guidance, mentorship, and professional development.

Oversee the end-to-end systems engineering lifecycle, from requirements definition and analysis to system design, integration, and validation of complex mission-critical public safety systems

Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including product management, software development, and hardware engineering, to define system architectures and technical roadmaps.

Ensure adherence to industry standards, best practices, and internal processes for systems engineering.

Drive continuous improvement initiatives to enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of the systems engineering function.

Manage project timelines, resources, and budgets for systems engineering activities.

Represent the systems engineering team in customer interactions and technical discussions.

Conduct performance reviews, identify training needs, and foster a collaborative and high-performing team environment.

Manage operational and fiscal activities, including staffing levels, budgets, and financial goals

Accountable for and contribute towards profitability and/or cost containment by indirectly impacting the P&L through project work, tools development, and/or functional management

Regularly interface with senior Motorola leadership, key internal/external organisations and governmental bodies

Coordinate activities and direction through highly matrixed relationships

Manage activities with complex or conflicting legal requirements, technology requirements, or cultures

Adept at guiding, enabling and mentoring team members

Recognised by peers and key work partners for engineering expertise, knowledge of business needs, knowledge of customer operations, and the ability to guide resources to obtain objectives

Responsible for customer satisfaction and quality of engineering deliverables within the scope of assigned activities

Responsible for the productivity and professional growth of assigned employees

Active participation in Bid reviews (System Integration cost estimates) and Presales Engagement

Qualifications

Bachelors or Masters degree in Systems Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field.

Minimum of 10 years of experience in systems engineering, with at least 3 years in a leadership or management role.

Strong understanding of systems engineering principles, methodologies, and tools.

Proven experience in leading and developing high-performing engineering teams.

Excellent communication, interpersonal, and presentation skills.

Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced and dynamic environment.

Experience with mission-critical communication systems or public safety technologies is highly desirable.

Benefits

Motorola Solutions offers a comprehensive benefits package, including competitive salary, health insurance, paid time off, and opportunities for professional growth and development.

Motorola Solutions embraces a diverse pool of candidates in its recruitment activities. We consider all qualified applicants and do not discriminate based on age, gender identity, biological sex, sexual orientation, intersex status, race, national or ethnic origin, religion, disabilities or health conditions, marital or family status and other areas of potential difference.

Positon title: Senior Staff Systems Engineer

Location: South Australia

Hours: 37.5 Hours Per Week (Full time)

Salary Range: $150,000 - 175,000 AUD

Company Description

About SEEK

SEEK’s portfolio of diverse businesses make a positive impact on a truly global scale. Our purpose is to help people live more fulfilling and productive working lives and help organisations succeed. We create world-class technology solutions to connect more people to relevant employment, education, small business and volunteer opportunities. We have a culture of high-performance in our workplaces and celebrate the diversity of our employees who contribute to the success of our organisation.

Life at SEEK

SEEK’s purpose is at the centre of everything we do. Our SEEK, which defines the way we work, is all about what makes us unique and a little bit different. Passion, Team, Delivery and Future are our principles that drive innovation and creativity. SEEK strives to support employee wellbeing by providing an amazing experience at work which led us to being named AFR BOSS Top 10 Best Places to Work in Technology (2021-2024). We are proud to work in an environment that's inclusive where everyone's unique ideas, experiences and perspectives are valued.

Our award-winning head office in Cremorne (just a 4 min walk from Richmond station) provides an exceptional space to collaborate with colleagues. The building provides sweeping views of the city, a games area, sit and stand desks at every workstation, modern end-of-trip facilities and Thursday night drinks which gives our people an opportunity to connect in a social setting.

Job Description

The team

At SEEK, our AI Platform Services (AIPS) teams build platforms and solutions that solve complex information problems and enable AI innovation across the SEEK Group. Teams are cross-functional, multi-disciplinary squads that work within an APAC matrix organisational structure. The AI Platform Engineering teams have a specific mission to provide shared infrastructure, governance, and reusable assets that empower AI and product teams to experiment safely, ship faster, and continuously deliver intelligent, differentiated experiences at scale.

You will work with roles across the engineering spectrum from graduate to lead level, including Software Engineers, Engineering Managers, Heads of Engineering, Software Architects, Data Scientists, ML Engineers, Platform Engineers, Product Managers, Business Analysts, and Iteration Managers.

About the role 

A Staff Platform Engineer is a technical lead and senior member within our AI Platform Services (AIPS) Engineering teams. You'll be responsible for the technical strategy and direction of SEEK's GenAI and ML Platforms, ensuring these systems are highly available, resilient, and enable AI innovation at scale. Embedded within a team, you'll lead cross-team technical initiatives that influence AI platform strategy across AIPS and SEEK more broadly. Your primary focus is designing and building AI platform solutions that enable Data Scientists, Engineers, and product teams to deliver AI-powered experiences rapidly and safely. You bring in-depth technical understanding of AI platform infrastructure, multi-cloud architectures (AWS, GCP), and the operational requirements of GenAI and ML systems, while driving alignment of your team's technical roadmap to SEEK's overall AI strategy.
 

Responsibilities 

Design and evolve technical architecture for GenAI and ML platform systems, ensuring they are well-architected, highly available, and aligned with SEEK's development practices and technical metrics
Drive technical strategy that enables SEEK's AI innovation at scale, leading architecture decisions for multi-cloud AI infrastructure (AWS, GCP) and aligning platform capabilities with business objectives
Build and operate highly scalable, performant AI infrastructure using AWS and Python for platform automation, tooling, and integrations while exemplifying clean, well-tested, secure code
Lead cross-team technical initiatives that influence AI platform strategy across AIPS, serving as the key point of contact for technical solutions and system interfaces
Partner with Data Scientists and ML Engineers to enable self-service model development, deployment, and operation while strengthening engineering discipline through understanding of AI/ML operational requirements
Support the Engineering Manager in capacity planning, risk management, and scoping of technical work while building strong relationships with Architecture, Security, and other AI platform teams
Grow team member technical capabilities through coaching on AI/ML platform patterns and practices while fostering a culture of safety, inclusivity, and diversity
Qualifications

Skills and experience 

Proven experience as a Staff Engineer, Principal Engineer, or similar technical leadership role building and operating production platform systems at scale
Strong expertise in cloud infrastructure and platform engineering, with deep hands-on experience in AWS services and multi-cloud architectures
Advanced Python programming skills with demonstrated experience building platform automation, tooling, and integrations for highly scalable systems
Experience with infrastructure-as-code (Terraform), container orchestration (Kubernetes), and observability tools for platform operations
Track record of leading technical roadmaps and driving platform health initiatives aligned with key technical metrics and SRE principles
Experience building platforms that enable self-service capabilities for engineering and data teams, with understanding of developer experience and platform adoption
Hands-on MLOps or ML Engineering experience, GenAI/LLM platform knowledge (model inference, vector databases, RAG patterns), GCP/Vertex AI experience, or ML lifecycle tooling expertise are good to have, but not a must.
Additional Information

Permanent Perks
At SEEK we offer:

Annual Performance Bonus Plan
Support of flexible working, including a mix of office and work from home days depending on your role.
Paid and unpaid leave benefits including Personal Flexi Days and Volunteer Days, as well as the opportunity to purchase additional leave
Support for parents with 14 weeks paid primary carers leave and 2 weeks paid leave for partners
The opportunity to work from anywhere for up to 4 weeks per financial year

At SEEK, we are passionate about fostering a culture of inclusion and wellbeing that embraces and values the diversity of our people. We are a purpose driven business that works with heart.

We know teams with diverse ideas, experiences and perspectives are more creative and are critical to ensuring effective delivery and innovating to enable our future success. As such, we welcome applications from people with diverse backgrounds and life experiences, especially as they relate to gender, sexual identity, culture, faith, disability and life stages. If you have the skills, curiosity and an adaptable mindset but don't meet every responsibility or qualification listed in this advertisement, please still get in touch with us.

Should you require any specific support or adjustments throughout the recruitment process and beyond, please advise us and we will be happy to assist.

For this role, only those candidates with the eligible right to work will be considered.

SEEK kindly requests no unsolicited resumes or approaches from recruitment agencies and will not be responsible for any associated fees.
